skill.name: torchdrug
skill.description: TorchDrug guidance for graph-based drug discovery, including molecular property prediction, protein modeling, knowledge graph reasoning, molecular generation, retrosynthesis, datasets, and model selection.
skill.summary: Build TorchDrug workflows for molecular, protein, and biomedical graph learning tasks.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, script, weak-crypto, sensitive-file, C2, and network indicators. Context review found documentation, Python examples, dataset names, ML terminology, and official TorchDrug links, with no prompt injection, credential access, exfiltration, or executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown examples and API names for installing and using TorchDrug. They do not execute automatically and do not concatenate user input into a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are inside fenced code blocks or parameter lists. The skill contains no runnable hook, script, or automation path that would execute these commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive Dynamic Evaluation Signal
low_findings.description: The eval finding points to task.eval(), a PyTorch evaluation-mode method in a training loop. It is not JavaScript or Python eval() on dynamic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding training loop uses task.train(), task.eval(), prediction, and metric evaluation. This confirms normal ML model evaluation rather than dynamic code execution.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Links Only
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded links to TorchDrug documentation and its GitHub repository. No code sends data to these URLs or performs network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are resource references in the Markdown resources section. There is no fetch, curl, telemetry, or credential transmission behavior in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Domain Terms Misclassified as Security Indicators
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file, C2, reconnaissance, and weak-cryptography alerts map to biomedical dataset names, graph terminology, relation examples, and ML loss names. No evidence found for credential access, command-and-control behavior, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed contexts describe SAMPL, HIV, ZINC, TransE, RotatE, BCE, and related scientific concepts. These are legitimate TorchDrug domain references, not security-sensitive operations.

content.user_title: Build Drug Discovery Models with TorchDrug
content.value_statement: Drug discovery workflows need careful model, dataset, and task choices. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through TorchDrug patterns for biomedical graph learning.
actual_capabilities: Selects TorchDrug datasets for molecular, protein, knowledge graph, generation, and retrosynthesis tasks.
Maps drug discovery goals to TorchDrug task classes and evaluation metrics.
Compares GNN, protein, knowledge graph, and generative model architectures.
Explains training workflows for property prediction, protein modeling, and link prediction.
Provides guidance for molecule generation, validation, and retrosynthesis planning.
limitations: Does not run TorchDrug experiments or train models by itself.
Does not validate generated molecules in wet labs or clinical settings.
Requires users to provide datasets, compute resources, and domain constraints.
May need updates when TorchDrug APIs or supported datasets change.

use_cases.title: Plan a Molecular Property Pipeline
use_cases.description: Choose datasets, models, loss functions, and metrics for ADMET, toxicity, solubility, or activity prediction.

use_cases.title: Compare Protein Modeling Approaches
use_cases.description: Select sequence or structure models for protein function, stability, localization, or interaction tasks.

use_cases.title: Design Biomedical Graph Experiments
use_cases.description: Use knowledge graph completion, generation, or retrosynthesis references to structure experimental workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Starting Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a TorchDrug workflow for my drug discovery task. My data type is [molecules/proteins/knowledge graph/reactions], and my goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Select Dataset and Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a defined prediction target
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend TorchDrug datasets, splitting strategies, losses, and metrics for predicting [property] from [input data]. Explain tradeoffs and validation risks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Architectures
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare TorchDrug model options for [task]. Include GIN, GAT, SchNet, GearNet, ESM, RotatE, or other relevant models.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Experiment Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my TorchDrug experiment design for [task]. Check data leakage, model choice, metrics, chemical validity, scalability, and domain validation steps.

output_examples.input: I need to predict blood-brain barrier penetration for small molecules.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends BBBP, scaffold splitting, a GIN baseline, binary cross-entropy, and AUROC plus AUPRC reporting.

output_examples.input: I want to model protein function from sequences without structures.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests sequence models such as ESM or ProteinBERT, transfer learning, small learning rates, and function-specific metrics.

output_examples.input: I need a retrosynthesis workflow for candidate molecules.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines center identification, synthon completion, route ranking, commercial availability checks, and expert chemistry review.

best_practices: Start with the smallest relevant dataset before scaling to large biomedical corpora.
Use scaffold splits and held-out test sets for realistic molecular evaluation.
Validate model outputs with domain rules and expert review before acting on predictions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated molecules or routes as experimentally proven results.
Do not compare models using random splits when scaffold splits are required.
Do not ignore missing labels, class imbalance, or data leakage in biomedical datasets.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps users design TorchDrug workflows for molecular, protein, reaction, and biomedical graph learning tasks.

faq.question: Does it install or run TorchDrug?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users must run installation and experiments in their own environment.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it generate drug candidates?
faq.answer: It explains TorchDrug generation workflows, but users must train models and validate candidates separately.

faq.question: Is it limited to molecules?
faq.answer: No. It also covers proteins, knowledge graphs, retrosynthesis, and model architecture selection.

faq.question: What safety review was performed?
faq.answer: Static alerts were reviewed in context. No malicious intent, prompt injection, exfiltration, or executable skill code was found.
